title: grants — queued jobs
description: Six queued jobs. Retention + reconciliation live on the `retention` queue (off-peak, batched). Notification + cache invalidation live on `notifications` (near-real-time). Bulk provisioning on `bulk` (isolated so a large upload cannot starve other work).

jobs.description: Sweeps grants where expires_at <= now() AND revoked_at IS NULL. For each: set revoked_at = expires_at, revoked_by = null, revoked_reason = 'expired'. Dispatches GrantExpired per row (afterCommit). Batched — max 500 per run to avoid blocking the queue.

Sweeps grants where expires_at BETWEEN now() AND now() + 24 hours AND revoked_at IS NULL AND metadata->'expiring_notification_sent' IS NULL. Dispatches GrantExpiringSoonNotification to subject + grantor. Marks metadata.expiring_notification_sent = now() to prevent double-send. Runs daily; picks up any grant that will expire within 24h.

Hard-deletes grants where revoked_at < now() - 90 days AND decision='allow'. Deny grants are retained INDEFINITELY (design decision: an inviolable block that was ever issued is compliance-worthy even after revoke — someone might need to know 'this user was once explicitly blocked from this resource'). Batched; runs daily 04:00 UTC per schedule.

Sweeps active grants (revoked_at IS NULL) and verifies the resource_type + resource_id still resolves to a live row. When the resource has been hard-deleted out-of-band (should be rare — tenant cascade covers most cases), revoke the grant with revoked_reason='orphaned_resource' + fire GrantResourceOrphaned. Runs weekly. Batched — sweeps up to 10000 grants per run.

Processes a bulk-provision CSV batch. Reads uploaded CSV row-by-row, calls GrantIssuer::issue() for each; captures per-row success + failure into a batch report. Fires BulkGrantIssued once when finished. Isolated queue so a slow bulk upload does not stall other grant activity.

input.csv_object_key: string (S3 / GCS key for the uploaded file)

jobs.description: Fired by grant lifecycle events (Issued / Updated / Revoked / Expired) to invalidate the L1 in-process cache (per-worker) + L2 Redis cache entries for the affected (tenant, subject_type, subject_id) tuple. Also invalidates any (tenant, resource_type, resource_id) reverse-cached entries. Rate-limited per (subject_id) to 1 invalidation per 100ms so a burst of grant changes doesn't thrash the cache.

notifications.description: Grant lifecycle notifications + cache invalidation. Near-real-time; high priority.

retention.description: Batched sweep jobs (expire, purge, reconcile). Runs off-hours per schedule.json.

bulk.description: Bulk CSV processing. Isolated because a large upload (10k+ rows) should never stall other grant activity.
